title: Heat and moisture exchange media
abstract: This invention relates to heat and moisture exchange (HME) materials, particularly for use in heat and moisture exchange with respiratory gases, and methods for their manufacture. The invention provides an improved HME material (10a) comprising a plurality of discrete protrusions (510), as opposed to continuous ridges or corrugations, extending from at least part of one surface of the HME material (10a), and to an associated manufacturing method.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . An HME material comprising a plurality of discrete protrusions extending from at least part of one surface of the HME material wherein each protrusion is completely surrounded by substantially non-protruding HME material. 2. An HME material according to Claim 1 , wherein the protrusions are formed from the HME material. 3. An HME material according any preceding claim, wherein the regions of the HME material from which each protrusion is formed has a maximum dimension of between 0.4 mm and 5.0 mm, between 0.6 mm and 3.0 mm or between 0.8 mm and 2.0 mm. 4. An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein the protrusions do not span the entire width of the HME material. 5. An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein the protrusions are hemispherical, frustoconical, cylindrical, pyramidal, cuboid, hemicylindrical or any combination of these. 6. An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein the protrusions protrude from the surface of the HME material by between 0.1 mm and 1 .0 mm, between 0.3 mm and 0.8 mm or between 0.5 mm and 0.6 mm. 7. An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein the protrusions are distributed regularly on the surface of the HME material. 8. An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein the density of the protrusions per cm 2 is between 3 and 50, between 5 and 40, between 8 and 30 or between 10 and 20. 9. An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein each protrusion comprises a perforation in the HME material. 10. An HME material according to Claim 9, wherein the perforations have a maximum dimension of between 0.4 mm and 5.0 mm, between 0.6 mm and 3.0 mm or between 0.8 mm and 2.0 mm. 1 1 . An HME material according to Claim 9 or 10, wherein a portion of the HME material around the edge of each perforation protrudes from the surface of the HME material. 12. An HME material according to any of Claims 9 to 10, wherein the perforations are cuts in the HME material that partially surround a region of HME material, such that that region of HME material forms a flap extending from the surface of the HME material. 13. An HME material according to Claim 12, wherein the cut is generally C- shaped such that the flap is substantially circular. 14. An HME material according to any preceding claims, wherein the HME material comprises cellulose paper. 15. An HME material according to Claim 14, wherein the HME material further comprises natural fibres such as cotton, or synthetic fibres such as polyamine, polyester, polyurethane, polyacrylonitrile and polyvinyl alcohol, or any combination of these. 1 6. An HME material according to any preceding claims, wherein the HME material comprises a hygroscopic material. 17. An HME material according to Claim 1 6, wherein the hygroscopic material comprises polyols such as glycols and glycerine, hygroscopic polymers such as polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylic acid and polyvinyl alcohol, and hygroscopic salts 

 such as polyacrylate, calcium chloride, potassium chloride and lithium chloride, or any combination of the above. 18. An HME material according to any preceding claims, wherein the HME material has a thickness of between 0.01 mm and 1 .0 mm, between 0.05 mm and 0.5 mm, between 0.1 mm and 0.3 mm, or about 0.2 mm. 19. An HME material according to any preceding claims, wherein the HME material is in the form of elongate strips. 20. An HME material according to Claim 19, wherein the width of the strip of HME material is between 5 mm and 20 mm or between 8 mm and 18 mm. 21 . An HME material according to any preceding claim, wherein discrete protrusions extend from at least a part of two opposing surfaces of the HME material. 22. An HME medium comprising an HME material according to any preceding claim. 23. The HME medium of Claim 22, wherein the HME medium comprises a plurality of overlying layers of the HME material. 24. The HME medium of Claim 23, wherein the HME medium is in the form of a coil of the HME material. 25. The HME medium of Claim 24, wherein the HME medium is between 1 .5 cm and 8.0 cm in diameter. 26. An HME device comprising a HME medium according to any of Claims 22 to 25. 27. A method of embossing an HME material comprising urging an HME material against an embossing surface comprising a plurality of discrete projections. 28. The method of Claim 27, wherein the HME material is urged against the projections on the embossing surface by a second embossing surface. 29. The method of Claim 28, wherein the second embossing surface comprises recesses that correspond to the projections of the first embossing surface. 30. The method of Claim 29, wherein the second embossing surface further comprises projections that correspond to recesses provided in the first embossing surface. 31 . The method of any of Claims 28 to 30, wherein the first and second embossing surfaces are circumferential surfaces of first and second embossing rollers. 32. The method of Claim 31 , wherein the first and second embossing rollers define a nip at which the circumferential surfaces of the rollers come into contact. 33. The method of Claim 32, wherein the projections of the first embossing roller mesh with the recesses of the second embossing roller at the nip. 34. The method of any of Claims 27 to 33, wherein the projections form perforations in the HME material. 35. The method of any of Claims 27 to 34, wherein the height of the projections is between 0.2mm and 6.0mm, between 0.4mm and 4.0mm or between 0.6mm and 2.0mm. 36. The method of any of Claim 27 to 35, wherein the height of the projections may be between 1 and 30 times, between 2 and 20 times or between 3 and 10 times the thickness of the HME material. 37. The method of any of Claim 27 to 36, wherein the projections are in the form of a cylinder having a hemispherical end. 38. The method of Claim 37, wherein the cylinder has a diameter that is greater than its height. 39. The method of Claim 37, wherein the cylinder has a height that is greater than its diameter. 40. The method of any of Claim 27 to 36, wherein the projections are in the form of a cylinder in which the surface of the distal end is inclined relative to its longitudinal axis. 41 . The method of any of Claims 27 to 40 further comprising winding the embossed HME material into a coil.
